Sensitivity Experiment

Steak Tissue BioZ
at 4 kHz

MAX30001G Evaluation Kit  ·  Before & After 0.2 mL Saline Injection

DateJuly 3, 2026
Frequency4 kHz
Injected volume0.2 mL saline
TissueBeef steak (ex-vivo)

Purpose

This test checks whether the MAX30001G EV Kit can detect a small volume change (0.2 mL of saline) in meat tissue at 4 kHz. Saline mimics the ionic conductivity change that would occur during breastfeeding. A measurable impedance drop confirms the device has sufficient sensitivity for the main experiment.

Result

79.9 Ω
Before — mean |Z|
71.6 Ω
After — mean |Z|
-8.3 Ω
ΔZ (absolute)
-10.3%
ΔZ (relative)

A 0.2 mL saline injection produced a clear impedance drop of 8.3 Ω (10.3%) at 4 kHz. The shift is well above the measurement noise (±2.2 Ω before, ±2.7 Ω after), confirming the device has adequate sensitivity to detect small fluid volume changes in tissue.

Setup overview — MAX30001G EV Kit with electrodes inserted in steak

Saline Injection

0.2 mL saline drawn in syringe

BioZ Time Series

Both recordings on the same 16.2s timeline. The shaded region (0–0.6s) is the transient settling period. Dashed lines show the stable mean for each condition.
